<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136720595 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00863: PV-2 Project Timelines

- page_id: `136720595`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136720595/PV-2+Project+Timelines
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:38.308+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Project viewpoint
- labels: ['pv-2', 'pv-2-project-timelines', 'pv-2-gantt-chart']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

305999026

305999028

305999027

**Description**

The PV-2 provides a timeline perspective on programs. The PV-2 is intended primarily to support the acquisition and fielding processes including the management of dependencies between projects and the integration of DoDD 5000.1 Defense Acquisition System policies to achieve a successfully integrated capability. The PV-2 is not limited to the acquisition and fielding processes.

The intended usage of the PV-2 includes:

- Project management and control (including delivery timescales).
- Project dependency risk identification.
- Management of dependencies.
- Portfolio management.

**Implementation**

PV-2 can be represented using a PV-2 diagram which is realized as a Gantt Chart.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Use of PV-2 should support the management of capability delivery and be aligned with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-3 Capability Phasing' space='UAF12P2024x'] model, if one exists.

305999025

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project' space='UAF12P2024x']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Status' space='UAF12P2024x']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Project Milestone' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Milestone Dependency' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Milestone' space='UAF12P2024x']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing PV-2 diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136720612 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00864: PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping

- page_id: `136720612`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136720612/PV-3+Project+to+Capability+Mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:38.655+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Project viewpoint
- labels: ['pv-3', 'pv-3-project-to-capability-mapping', 'pv-3-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

306143199

306143202

306143200

**Description**

The PV-3 supports the acquisition and deployment processes, including the management of dependencies between projects and the integration of all relevant project and program elements to achieve a capability.

The PV-3 maps programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives to capabilities to show how the specific elements help to achieve a capability. Programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives are mapped to the capability for a particular timeframe. Programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives may contribute to multiple capabilities and may mature across time. The analysis can be used to identify capability redundancies and shortfalls, highlight phasing issues, expose organizational or system interoperability problems, and support program decisions, such as when to phase out a legacy system.

The intended usage of the PV-3 includes:

- Tracing capability requirements to projects.
- Capability audit.

**Implementation**

PV-3 can be represented using a PV-3 diagram which is a non-editable Dependency Matrix. Capabilities are used as row elements and Actual Projects are used as column elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

This model is analogous to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix' space='UAF12P2024x'], but provides the interface between Capability and Actual Project models rather than Operational to System Models.

306143198

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Project' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Maps To Capability' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency' space='MD2024x']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating PV-3 matrix' space='UAF12P2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives are mapped to the capability for a particular timeframe. Programs, projects, portfolios, or initiatives may contribute to multiple capabilities and may mature across time. The analysis can be used to identify capability redundancies and shortfalls, highlight phasing issues, expose organizational or system interoperability problems, and support program decisions, such as when to phase out a legacy system.</p><p>The intended usage of the PV-3 includes:</p><ul><li>Tracing capability requirements to projects.</li><li>Capability audit.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>PV-3 can be represented using a PV-3 diagram which is a non-editable Dependency Matrix. Capabilities are used as row elements and Actual Projects are used as column elements.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping" ac:alt="PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping"><ri:attachment ri:filename="pv_3.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>This model is analogous to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-5a Operational Activity to System Function Traceability Matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, but provides the interface between Capability and Actual Project models rather than Operational to System Models.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="59709386-c38c-4256-bdd0-ea87e9076c2c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">306143198</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Capability" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Actual Project" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Maps To Capability" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Dependency" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating PV-3 matrix" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722564 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00865: Realized Operational Exchange wizard

- page_id: `136722564`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722564/Realized+Operational+Exchange+wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:23.051+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: ['realized-operational-exchange-wizard', 'unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1013711564

1013711566

1013711565

The **Realized Operational Exchange** wizard gives the instructions to follow when adding a new Operational Exchange or selecting one of the existing Operational Exchanges.

The **Realized Operational Exchange** wizard consists of two steps:

truenone

##### Operational Exchange creation (mandatory)

This step is designed to create a new Operational Exchange by specifying a Conveyed Item, both sending and receiving Operational Performers,and a direction of an Operational Exchange. Depending on a fact, if the selected Operational Performeris known or not, requested information differs:

- If sending and receiving Operational Performers are unknown from the wizard invocation context, you need to specify both. [ATTACHMENT filename='realized_operational_exchange_wizard_I_unknown_performers.png']
- If sending and receiving Operational Performers are known, they are added to a Realized Operational Exchange wizard automatically. [ATTACHMENT filename='realized_operational_exchange_wizard_I.png']

| Box | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Conveyed Item | One or more Operational Exchange Items that are conveyed by the information flow from the sending Operational Performers to receiving Operational Performers.Exchange KindConveyed ItemConfiguration ExchangeCapability ConfigurationEnergy ExchangeNatural ResourceGeo Political Extent ExchangeGeo Political Extent TypeInformation ExchangeInformation ElementOperational SignalMateriel ExchangeTechnologySoftwareResource ArtifactOrganizational ExchangeProjectOrganizationPostResponsibilityPerson |
| Exchange Kind | Conveyed Item |
| Configuration Exchange | Capability Configuration |
| Energy Exchange | Natural Resource |
| Geo Political Extent Exchange | Geo Political Extent Type |
| Information Exchange | Information ElementOperational Signal |
| Materiel Exchange | TechnologySoftwareResource Artifact |
| Organizational Exchange | ProjectOrganizationPostResponsibilityPerson |
| Operational Exchange | Operational Exchange that specifies an information flow for specified conveyed items.If you select an existing Operational Exchange from the drop-down list, the Conveyed Item and Direction boxes will be filled with corresponding information automatically. You will be able to edit the values of conveyed items.The direction of the information flow won’t be editable.Available if sending and receiving Operational Performers are known from the wizard invocation context. |
| Sending Operational Performers | Operational Performers that send the specified conveyed information.Available if an Operational Performer is unknown from the wizard invocation context. |
| Receiving Operational Performers | Operational Performers that receive the specified conveyed information.Available if an Operational Performer is unknown from the wizard invocation context. |
| Direction | The direction of the information flow.After changing the direction from the default one, the sending Operational Performer is switched with the receiving Operational Performer. The direction of the Operational Exchange has changed accordingly. |
| Add Operational Exchange to corresponding relationships | Select the check box if you want to add the Operational Exchange to corresponding relationships existing in other related diagrams. |

##### Producing and Consuming Activities editing

This step is designed to review Producing and Consuming Activities for the created or selected Operational Exchange. Operational Exchanges are produced and consumed by Activities acting under theNodesProducing and Consuming Activities specification (optional). You can remove the realization between listed Operational Activity pairs.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Affected diagram reviewing

In this step, you can see the list of diagrams where the selected Operational Exchange will be realized and displayed.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1013711563

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Operational Exchanges in L4 Logical Activities diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-5b diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-6c diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722571 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00866: Realized Resource Exchange wizard

- page_id: `136722571`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722571/Realized+Resource+Exchange+wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:23.526+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: ['realized-resource-interaction-wizard']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1013802005

1013802008

1013802006

To open the **Realized****Resource Exchange** wizard

- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UAF12P2024x'] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024x'] (DoDAF) diagrams: - On the diagram pane, select the Association and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_exchange.png'] . - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange.png'] and on the diagram pane, connect Resources. - Click the Control relationship and connect Posts or Organizations with Posts, Organizations, Natural Resources, Software, Resource Artifact, Technology. - Click the Command relationship and connect Posts, Organizations.
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description' space='UAF12P2024x'] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Internal Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024x'] (DoDAF): - On the diagram pane, select the Resource Connector and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . - On the diagram palette, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and then click the Resource Connector.

- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024x'] Flow (DoDAF) diagram: - On the diagram pane, select Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow and on the smart manipulator toolbar click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. - On the diagram palette, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and then click Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.

- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description' space='UAF12P2024x'] (DoDAF) diagram: - On the diagram pane, select the Resource Message and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . - On the diagram palette, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and then click the Resource Message.
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix' space='UAF12P2024x'] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-3a Systems-Services Matrix' space='UAF12P2024x'] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-3b Services-Services Matrix' space='UAF12P2024x'] (DoDAF) matrix : - On the diagram pane, right-click the intersection in the matrix and select Add New Resource Exchange .

The **Realized Resource****Exchange**wizard consists of two steps:

truenone

##### Resource Exchangecreation (mandatory)

This step is designed to create a new Resource Exchangeby specifying a conveyed Resource ExchangeItem, both sending and receiving Resources, and a direction of a Resource Exchange. Depending on a fact, if selected Resources are known or not, requested information differs:

- If sending and receiving Resources are unknown from the wizard invocation context, you need to specify both.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- If sending and receiving Resources are known, they are added to a Realized Resource **Exchange** wizard automatically.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Box | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Exchange Kind | Shows the type of exchanged resources:Resource Communication.Resource Movement.Resource Energy Flow.Geo Political Extent Exchange. |
| Conveyed Item | One or more Resource Exchange Items that are conveyed by the information flow from the sending Resource to receiving Resource.Exchange KindConveyed ItemGeo Political Extent ExchangeGeo Political Extent TypeResource CommunicationData ElementResource SignalResource Energy FlowNatural ResourceResource MovementSecurity EnclaveResource MitigationCapability ConfigurationSystemResource ArchitectureNatural ResourceTechnologySoftwareResource ArtifactProjectResponsibilityPersonPostOrganizationKnown Resource |
| Exchange Kind | Conveyed Item |
| Geo Political Extent Exchange | Geo Political Extent Type |
| Resource Communication | Data ElementResource Signal |
| Resource Energy Flow | Natural Resource |
| Resource Movement | Security EnclaveResource MitigationCapability ConfigurationSystemResource ArchitectureNatural ResourceTechnologySoftwareResource ArtifactProjectResponsibilityPersonPostOrganizationKnown Resource |
| Resource Exchange | Resource Exchange that specifies an information flow for specified conveyed items.If you select an existing Resource Exchange from the drop-down list, Conveyed Item and Direction boxes will be filled with corresponding information automatically. You will be able to edit values of conveyed items.The direction of the information flow won’t be editable.Available if sending and receiving Resources are known from the wizard invocation context.Resource Exchange TypeSending and Receiving ResourcesCommandOrganizationPersonPostResponsibilityControlNatural ResourceOrganizational ResourceResource Artifact:Capability ConfigurationResource MitigationSecurity EnclaveSystemResource ExchangePhysical ResourceResource ArchitectureKnown Resource |
| Resource Exchange Type | Sending and Receiving Resources |
| Command | OrganizationPersonPostResponsibility |
| Control | Natural ResourceOrganizational ResourceResource Artifact:Capability ConfigurationResource MitigationSecurity EnclaveSystem |
| Resource Exchange | Physical ResourceResource ArchitectureKnown Resource |
| Sending Resource | Resource that sends the specified conveyed information.Available if a Resource is unknown from the wizard invocation context. |
| Receiving Resource | Resource that receives the specified conveyed information.Available if a Resource is unknown from the wizard invocation context. |
| Direction | Direction of the information flow.After changing the direction from the default one, the sending resource will be switched with the receiving resource. The direction of the Resource Exchange will be changed accordingly.In the Direction drop-down list, you can choose the direction of flow, i.e. what type of element is the sender and what type of element is the receiver. The drop-down list is not applicable, if the source and target is the same element and direction could not be specified. |
| Add Resource Exchange to corresponding relationships | Select the check box, if you want to add the Resource Exchange to corresponding relationships existing in other related diagrams. These are the relationships that can realize the Resource Exchange: - Resource Connector (, )- Function Control Flow ()- Function Object Flow ()- Resource Message ()- Association (, )- Control (, )- Command (, )- Intersection in the matrix (, SvcV-3a , ) |

##### Producing and Consuming Functions editing

This step is designed to review Producing and Consuming Functions for the created or selected Resource Exchange. ResourceExchanges are produced and consumed by Activities acting under the Resource. You can remove the realization between listed Service Function pairs.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Affected diagram reviewing

In this step, you can see the list of diagrams where the selected Resource Exchange will be realized and displayed.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1013802003

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Resource Exchange in P1 diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Resource Exchange in P2 diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Resource Exchange in P4 Resource Functions diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P4 Resource Functions diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Resource Exchange in P6 diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Resource Exchange in SvcV-10c diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Resource Exchanges in SV-1 diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Resource Exchange in SV-1 Internal diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Resource Exchange in SV-2 Internal diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Resource Exchange in SV-2 diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Resource Exchange in SV-4 Systems Functionality Flow Description diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Resource Exchange in SV-10c diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Producing and Consuming Functions dialog' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024x']

and on the diagram pane, connect<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Resources.</span><br style="color: rgb(255,0,0);" />- Click the Control relationship and connect Posts or Organizations with Posts, Organizations, Natural Resources, Software, Resource Artifact, Technology.<br />- Click the Command relationship and connect Posts, Organizations.<br /><br /><br /></li><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1 Internal]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Internal Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-2 Internal]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (DoDAF):<br />- On the diagram pane, select the Resource Connector and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="New Resource Exchange " ac:alt="New Resource Exchange "><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>.<br />- On the diagram palette, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Resource Exchange "><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> and then click the Resource Connector.<br /><br /></li></ul><ul><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> Flow (DoDAF) diagram:<br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">- On the diagram pane, select Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow and on the smart manipulator toolbar click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="New Resource Exchange " ac:alt="New Resource Exchange "><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>. </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">- On the diagram palette, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Resource Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> and then click Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.<br /><br /></span></li></ul><ul><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-10c]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (DoDAF) diagram:<br />- On the diagram pane, select the Resource Message and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="New Resource Exchange " ac:alt="New Resource Exchange "><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>.<br />- On the diagram palette, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Resource Exchange "><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> and then click the Resource Message.<br /><br /></li><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-3]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SvcV-3a Systems-Services Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-3a]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SvcV-3b Services-Services Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-3b]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (DoDAF) matrix :<br />- On the diagram pane, right-click the intersection in the matrix and select <strong>Add New Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange</span></strong>.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>The <strong>Realized Resource </strong><strong>Exchange </strong>wizard consists of two steps:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f88aef8d-1a7b-4fda-b7a2-c72f73194f16"><ac:parameter ac:name="outline">true</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="style">none</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>Resource <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Exchange</span><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span>creation (mandatory)</h3><p>This step is designed to create a new Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>by specifying a conveyed Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>Item, both sending and receiving Resources, and a direction of a Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange. Depending on a fact, if selected Resources are known or not, requested information differs:</span></p><ul><li>If sending and receiving Resources are unknown from the wizard invocation context, you need to specify both.</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_resource_exchange_new_noSR.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ul><li>If sending and receiving Resources are known, they are added to a <strong>Realized Resource </strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Exchange </strong></span>wizard automatically.</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_resource_exchange_new.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><table class="wrapped relative-table" style="width: 85.6829%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 13.6398%;" /><col style="width: 86.3602%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Box</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><th colspan="1">Exchange Kind</th><td colspan="1"><p><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span>Shows the type of exchanged resources:</p><ul><li>Resource Communication.</li><li>Resource Movement.</li><li>Resource Energy Flow.</li><li>Geo Political Extent Exchange.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>Conveyed Item</th><td><p>One or more Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>Items that are conveyed by the information flow from the sending Resource to receiving Resource.</p><table class="wrapped"><tbody><tr><th>Exchange Kind</th><th>Conveyed Item</th></tr><tr><td>Geo Political Extent Exchange</td><td><ul><li>Geo Political Extent Type</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Resource Communication</td><td colspan="1"><ul><li>Data Element</li><li>Resource Signal</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Resource Energy Flow</td><td colspan="1"><ul><li>Natural Resource</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Resource Movement</td><td colspan="1"><ul><li>Security Enclave</li><li>Resource Mitigation</li><li>Capability Configuration</li><li>System</li><li>Resource Architecture</li><li>Natural Resource</li><li>Technology</li><li>Software</li><li>Resource Artifact</li><li>Project</li><li>Responsibility</li><li>Person</li><li>Post</li><li>Organization</li><li>Known Resource</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table></td></tr><tr><th>Resource Exchange</th><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>that specifies an information flow for specified conveyed items.</p><p>If you select an existing Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>from the drop-down list, Conveyed Item and Direction boxes will be filled with corresponding information automatically. You will be able to edit values of conveyed items.</p><p>The direction of the information flow won’t be editable.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="53b7e3bc-c72c-4db3-96f4-7cfc0283caad"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Available if sending and receiving Resources are known from the wizard invocation context.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><tbody><tr><th>Resource Exchange Type</th><th>Sending and Receiving  Resources</th></tr><tr><td>Command</td><td><ul><li>Organization</li><li>Person</li><li>Post</li><li>Responsibility</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>Control</td><td><ul><li>Natural Resource</li><li>Organizational Resource</li><li>Resource Artifact:<ul><li>Capability Configuration</li><li>Resource Mitigation</li><li>Security Enclave</li><li>System</li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>Resource Exchange</td><td><ul><li>Physical Resource</li><li>Resource Architecture</li><li>Known Resource</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table></div></td></tr><tr><th>Sending Resource</th><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Resource that sends the specified conveyed information.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4060d829-b8dd-41cd-a13c-378b0e2fcc51"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Available if a Resource is unknown from the wizard invocation context.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><th>Receiving Resource</th><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p> Resource that receives the specified conveyed information.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8bc5aafa-d3f6-4eb1-83a2-6a8cf1305629"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Available if a Resource is unknown from the wizard invocation context.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><th> Direction</th><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p> Direction of the information flow.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Direction of the information flow" ac:alt="Direction of the information flow"><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_resource_exchange_wizard_direction.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>After changing the direction from the default one, the sending resource will be switched with the receiving resource. The direction of the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>will be changed accordingly.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="39279679-a8b2-4d7f-b081-e4b603107ee8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>In the <strong>Direction</strong> drop-down list, you can choose the direction of flow, i.e. what type of element is the sender and what type of element is the receiver. The drop-down list is not applicable, if the source and target is the same element and direction could not be specified.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><th><p>Add Resource Exchange to corresponding relationships</p></th><td><p>Select the check box, if you want to add the Resource <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>to corresponding relationships existing in other related diagrams. These are the relationships that can realize the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange:<br /></span></p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">- Resource Connector (<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1 Internal]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Internal Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-2 Internal]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>)</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">- Function Control Flow (<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>)</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">- Function Object Flow (<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>)</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">- Resource Message (<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-10c Systems Event-Trace Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-10c]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>)</span></p><p>- <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Association</span></span></span> (<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>)</p><p>- Control (<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span>)</p><p>- Command (<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span>)</p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">- Intersection in the matrix <span class="confluence-link">(<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-3]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SvcV-3a Systems-Services Matrix" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">SvcV-3a</span></ac:link-body></ac:link><span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link"> </span>, <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SvcV-3b Services-Services Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-3b]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>)</span></span></td></tr></tbody></table><h3><br />Producing and Consuming Functions editing</h3><p>This step is designed to review Producing and Consuming Functions for the created or selected Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange. Resource </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges</span> are produced and consumed by Activities acting under the Resource. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can remove the realization between listed Service Function pairs.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Specifying Producing and Consuming Functions in Realized Resource Exchange wizard" ac:alt="Specifying Producing and Consuming Functions in Realized Resource Exchange wizard"><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_resource_exchange_wizard_step2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Affected diagram reviewing</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In this step, you can see the list of diagrams where the selected Resource Exchange will be realized and displayed.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_resource_exchange_wizard_III.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0d095e42-7b02-4b7b-8d21-5655ec088c53"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1013802003</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating Resource Exchange in P1 diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating Resource Exchange in P2 diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating Resource Exchange in SvcV-10c diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating Resource Exchanges in SV-1 diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page Exchange in SV-2 Internal diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating Resource Exchange in SV-2 diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating Resource Exchange in SV-4 Systems Functionality Flow Description diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating Resource Exchange in SV-10c diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Producing and Consuming Functions dialog" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722582 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00867: Realized Service Exchange wizard

- page_id: `136722582`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722582/Realized+Service+Exchange+wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:23.923+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: ['realized-service-interaction-wizard']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1013802005

INLINE

1013802008

INLINE

1013802006

INLINE

To open the **Realized****Service Exchange** wizard

- Do one of the following: - On the diagram pane, select the Service Association and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_exchange.png'] . - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='service_exchange_button.png'] and on the diagram pane, connect Services.

The **Realized Service****Exchange**wizard consists of two steps:

truenone

##### Service Exchangecreation (mandatory)

This step is designed to create a new Service Exchangeby specifying a conveyed Service ExchangeItem, both sending and receiving Services and a direction of a Service Exchange. Depending on a fact, if selected Services are known or not, requested information differs:

- If sending and receiving Services are unknown from the wizard invocation context, you need to specify both. [ATTACHMENT filename='realized_service_exchange_unknown_services_step_I.png']
- If sending and receiving Services are known , they are added to a Realized Service **Exchange** wizard automatically. [ATTACHMENT filename='realized_service_exchange_known_services_step_I.png']

| Box | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Exchange Kind | Shows the type of exchanged items:Information ExchangeConfiguration ExchangeEnergy ExchangeMateriel ExchangeOrganizational Exchange |
| Conveyed Item | One or more Service Exchange Items to be conveyed by the information flow from the sending Service to receiving Service.Exchange KindConveyed ItemInformation ExchangeService SignalOperational InformationConfiguration ExchangeResource ArchitectureEnergy ExchangeNatural ResourceMateriel ExchangeResource ArtifactOrganizational ExchangeOrganizational Resources |
| Exchange Kind | Conveyed Item |
| Information Exchange | Service SignalOperational Information |
| Configuration Exchange | Resource Architecture |
| Energy Exchange | Natural Resource |
| Materiel Exchange | Resource Artifact |
| Organizational Exchange | Organizational Resources |
| Service Exchange | Service Exchange that specifies an information flow for specified conveyed items.If you select an existing Service Exchange from the drop-down list, Conveyed Item and Direction boxes will be filled with corresponding information automatically. You will be able to edit the values of conveyed items.The direction of the information flow won’t be editable.Available, if sending and receiving Services are known from the wizard invocation context. |
| Sending Service | Service that sends the specified conveyed information.Available, if a Service is unknown from the wizard invocation context. |
| Receiving Service | Service that receives the specified conveyed information.Available, if a Service is unknown from the wizard invocation context. |
| Direction | The direction of the information flow.After changing the direction from the default one, the sending resource will be switched with the receiving resource. The direction of the Resource Exchange will be changed accordingly.In the Direction drop-down list, you can choose the direction of flow, i.e. what type of element is the sender and what type of element is the receiver. The drop-down list is not applicable if the source and target are the same element and the direction cannot be specified. |
| Add Service Exchange to corresponding relationships | Select the check box, if you want to add the Service Exchange to corresponding relationships existing in other related diagrams. |

##### Producing and Consuming Service Functions editing

This step is designed to review Producing and Consuming Service Functions for the created or selected Service Exchange. ServiceExchanges are produced and consumed by Service Functions acting under the Services. You can remove the realization between listed Service Function pairs.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Affected diagram reviewing

In this step, you can see the list of diagrams where the selected Service Exchange will be realized and displayed.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1013802003

INLINE

ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a9fce253-9fad-4a4a-a922-748068032cac"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1013802008</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="68340798-bd94-41ed-8689-20b310871f8a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1013802006</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To open the <span class="confluence-link"><strong>Realized </strong><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Service Exchange</strong></span> wizard</span></p><hr /><ul><li>Do one of the following:<br />- On the diagram pane, select the Service <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="9e6f3b3e-f326-4707-8b40-a56452b8895f">Association</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></span></span> and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="New Resource Exchange" ac:alt="New Resource Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.<br />- Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="service_exchange_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Realized Service Exchange wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and on the diagram pane, connect<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Services.</span></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>The <strong>Realized Service </strong><strong>Exchange </strong>wizard consists of two steps:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1a0535f1-b1e5-4019-9bd3-88ba03d66eab"><ac:parameter ac:name="outline">true</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="style">none</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>Service <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Exchange</span><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span>creation (mandatory)</h3><p>This step is designed to create a new Service <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>by specifying a conveyed Service <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>Item, both sending and receiving Services and a direction of a Service <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange. Depending on a fact, if selected Services are known or not, requested information differs:</span></p><ul><li>If sending and receiving Services are <u>unknown</u> from the wizard invocation context, you need to specify both.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_service_exchange_unknown_services_step_I.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Realized Service Exchange wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>If sending and receiving Services are <u>known</u>, they are added to a <strong>Realized Service </strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Exchange </strong></span>wizard automatically.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_service_exchange_known_services_step_I.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Realized Service Exchange wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><table class="wrapped relative-table" style="width: 85.6829%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 13.6398%;" /><col style="width: 86.3602%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Box</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><th colspan="1">Exchange Kind</th><td colspan="1"><p><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span>Shows the type of exchanged items:</p><ul><li>Information Exchange</li><li>Configuration Exchange</li><li>Energy Exchange</li><li>Materiel Exchange</li><li>Organizational Exchange</li></ul></td></tr><tr><th>Conveyed Item</th><td><p>One or more Service <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>Items to be conveyed by the information flow from the sending Service to receiving Service.</p><table class="wrapped"><tbody><tr><th>Exchange Kind</th><th>Conveyed Item</th></tr><tr><td>Information Exchange</td><td><ul><li>Service Signal</li><li>Operational Information</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>Configuration Exchange</span></td><td colspan="1"><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Resource Architecture</span></li></ul></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>Energy Exchange</span></td><td colspan="1"><ul><li>Natural Resource</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>Materiel Exchange</span></td><td colspan="1"><ul><li>Resource Artifact</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span>Organizational Exchange</span></td><td colspan="1"><ul><li><span>Organizational Resources</span></li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table></td></tr><tr><th>Service Exchange</th><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Service <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>that specifies an information flow for specified conveyed items.</p><p>If you select an existing Service <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>from the drop-down list, Conveyed Item and Direction boxes will be filled with corresponding information automatically. You will be able to edit the values of conveyed items.</p><p>The direction of the information flow won’t be editable.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="53b7e3bc-c72c-4db3-96f4-7cfc0283caad"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Available, if sending and receiving Services are known from the wizard invocation context.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><th>Sending Service</th><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Service that sends the specified conveyed information.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4060d829-b8dd-41cd-a13c-378b0e2fcc51"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Available, if a Service is unknown from the wizard invocation context.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><th>Receiving Service</th><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Service that receives the specified conveyed information.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8bc5aafa-d3f6-4eb1-83a2-6a8cf1305629"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Available, if a Service is unknown from the wizard invocation context.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><th> Direction</th><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p> The direction of the information flow.<br /><ac:image ac:height="62"><ri:attachment ri:filename="change_direction_services.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Realized Service Exchange wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>After changing the direction from the default one, the sending resource will be switched with the receiving resource. The direction of the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>will be changed accordingly.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="39279679-a8b2-4d7f-b081-e4b603107ee8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>In the <strong>Direction</strong> drop-down list, you can choose the direction of flow, i.e. what type of element is the sender and what type of element is the receiver. The drop-down list is not applicable if the source and target are the same element and the direction cannot be specified.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><th><p>Add Service Exchange to corresponding relationships</p></th><td><p>Select th<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="90ad5b58-fda9-4350-8f4a-e2321079a0fe">e check box, if you want to add the Service </ac:inline-comment-marker><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="90ad5b58-fda9-4350-8f4a-e2321079a0fe">Exchange </ac:inline-comment-marker></span></span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="90ad5b58-fda9-4350-8f4a-e2321079a0fe">to corresponding relationships existing in other related diagrams.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p></td></tr></tbody></table><h3><br />Producing and Consuming Service Functions editing</h3><p>This step is designed to review Producing and Consuming Service Functions for the created or selected Service <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange. Service </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges</span> are produced and consumed by Service Functions acting under the Services. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can remove the realization between listed Service Function pairs.</span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_service_exchange_step_II.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Realized Service Exchange wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Affected diagram reviewing</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In this step, you can see the list of diagrams where the selected Service Exchange will be realized and displayed.</span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_service_exchange_wizard_III.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Realized Service Exchange wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2547aa68-66a4-4d18-886a-be19e4bd74d9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1013802003</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Services Connectivity diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Service Exchange Manager dialog" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SvcV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

1013802005

1013802008

INLINE

1013802006

To open the **Realized****Strategic Exchange** wizard

- Do one of the following: - On the diagram pane, select the Actual Enterprise Phase and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='strategic_exchange_button.png'] . - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='strategic_exchange_button_pallete.png'] and on the diagram pane, connect Actual Enterprise Phases.

The **Realized **Strategic******Exchange**wizard consists of only one step:

truenone

##### Strategic Exchangecreation

In this step, you create a new Strategic Exchangeby specifying its name, conveyed Item, and direction of a Strategic Exchange.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can choose one or more Conveyed Items from the list or create new ones. When creating a new Conveyed Item, choose the type depending on the viewpoint:

| Viewpoint | Conveyed Item Type |
| --- | --- |
| Strategic | Strategic Information |
| Security | Security Enclave |
| Resource Mitigation |  |
| Resources | System |
| Software |  |
| Resource Artifact |  |
| Technology |  |
| Natural Resource |  |
| Resource Service |  |
| Resource Architecture |  |
| Capability Configuration |  |
| Projects | Project |
| Personnel | Post |
|  | Responsibility |
|  | Person |
|  | Organization |
| Parameters | Geo Political Extent Type |
| Operational | Know Resource |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721378 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00869: Relating actual projects in NPV-1 diagram

- page_id: `136721378`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721378/Relating+actual+projects+in+NPV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:04.843+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF > NATO Programme Viewpoint > NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationships
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can relate actual projects to a sequence using the following relation types:

- Finish to Start. Target actual project cannot start earlier than the source actual project finishes.
- Start to Start. The target actual project cannot start earlier than the source actual project starts.
- Finish to Finish. The target actual project cannot finish earlier than the source actual project finishes.
- Start to Finish. The target actual project cannot finish earlier than the source actual project starts.

You can change actual project dates by moving an actual project across the timeline. The Actual Project Sequence validation rule is executed while changing the related project dates. If dates in the project sequence violate the rule, the relation is highlighted in red.

To relate actual projects

1. Select two actual projects.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Relate .
3. Select one of the available relation types:
  - Finish to Start
  - Start to Start
  - Finish to Finish
  - Start to Finish

To remove the relation between actual projects

1. Select two related actual projects.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Relate .
3. Select None .

To resolve the incorrect actual project sequence

1. On the highlighted relation shortcut menu, click Validate .
2. Select one of the suggested solutions.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If you change dates of an actual project containing actual sub projects, actual sub project dates change correspondingly.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can relate actual projects to a sequence using the following relation types:</p><ul><li>Finish to Start. Target actual project cannot start earlier than the source actual project finishes.</li><li>Start to Start. The target actual project cannot start earlier than the source actual project starts.</li><li>Finish to Finish. The target actual project cannot finish earlier than the source actual project finishes.</li><li>Start to Finish. The target actual project cannot finish earlier than the source actual project starts.</li></ul><p>You can change actual project dates by moving an actual project across the timeline. The Actual Project Sequence validation rule is executed while changing the related project dates. If dates in the project sequence violate the rule, the relation is highlighted in red.</p><p><br /></p><p>To relate actual projects</p><hr /><ol><li>Select two actual projects.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Relate</strong>.</li><li>Select one of the available relation types:<ul><li><strong>Finish to Start</strong></li><li><strong>Start to Start</strong></li><li><strong>Finish to Finish</strong></li><li><strong>Start to Finish</strong></li></ul></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation between actual projects</p><hr /><ol><li>Select two related actual projects.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Relate</strong>.</li><li>Select <strong>None</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To resolve the incorrect actual project sequence</p><hr /><ol><li>On the highlighted relation shortcut menu, click <strong>Validate</strong>.</li><li>Select one of the suggested solutions.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="NPV-1_relate.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Relating actual projects in NPV-1 diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="891e99ec-42d8-498a-9fe8-50471d67eb75"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you change dates of an actual project containing actual sub projects, actual sub project dates change correspondingly.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721610 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00870: Relating Projects in Lr diagram

- page_id: `136721610`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721610/Relating+Projects+in+Lr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:26.095+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > Lr Lines of Development > Manipulations in Lr Lines of Development. Lr Gantt chart
- labels: ['relate-project-in-lr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can relate projects to a sequence using the following relation types:

- Finish to Start. Target project cannot start earlier than the source project finishes.
- Start to Start. The target project cannot start earlier than the source project starts.
- Finish to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project finishes.
- Start to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project starts.

You can change project dates by moving a project across the timeline. The Actual Project Sequence validation rule is executed while changing the related project dates. If dates in the project sequence violate the rule, the relation is highlighted in red.

To relate projects

1. Select two Projects.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Relate .
3. Select one of the available relation types:
  - Finish to Start
  - Start to Start
  - Finish to Finish
  - Start to Finish

To remove the relation between projects

1. Select two related Projects.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Relate .
3. Select None .

To resolve the incorrect project sequence

1. On the highlighted relation shortcut menu, click Validate .
2. Select one of the suggested solutions. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] If you change dates of a Project containing Sub Projects, the Sub Project dates change correspondingly.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can relate projects to a sequence using the following relation types:</p><ul><li>Finish to Start. Target project cannot start earlier than the source project finishes.</li><li>Start to Start. The target project cannot start earlier than the source project starts.</li><li>Finish to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project finishes.</li><li>Start to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project starts.</li></ul><p>You can change project dates by moving a project across the timeline. The Actual Project Sequence validation rule is executed while changing the related project dates. If dates in the project sequence violate the rule, the relation is highlighted in red.</p><p> </p><p>To relate projects</p><hr /><ol><li>Select two Projects.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Relate</strong>.</li><li>Select one of the available relation types:<br /><ul><li><strong>Finish to Start</strong></li><li><strong>Start to Start</strong></li><li><strong>Finish to Finish</strong></li><li><strong>Start to Finish</strong></li></ul></li></ol><p> </p><p>To remove the relation between projects</p><hr /><ol><li>Select two related Projects.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Relate</strong>.</li><li>Select <strong>None</strong>.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To resolve the incorrect project sequence</p><hr /><ol><li>On the highlighted relation shortcut menu, click <strong>Validate</strong>.</li><li><p>Select one of the suggested solutions.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="PV-2_Validation.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Relating Projects in Lr diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1fb35907-6f21-414e-9f69-ae4a3c73a422"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you change dates of a Project containing Sub Projects, the Sub Project dates change correspondingly.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722739 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00871: Relationship mapping

- page_id: `136722739`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722739/Relationship+mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:32.851+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures > Importing from ArchiMate
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

195753003

195753014

195753004

How ArchiMate relationships are mapped to the UAF 1.2 relationships or added to UAF element properties after the import and the mapping rules are described in the following table:

| # | ArchiMate 3.1 relation | UAF 1.2 | Mapping Rule |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Name | Type |  |  |  |
| 1 | Access | ReadWriteAccessRead/Write | Flow Property | The ArchiMate Access is mapped to UAF Flow Property which is applied to Interface when the ArchiMate Access relation is used to relate the ArchiMate element to Business Interface/Technology Interface/Application Interface.Access Type is set as the direction for Flow Property: Read - in,Write - out,Access - inout,ReadWrite - inout.Also, see Mapping issues. |
|  | Dependency | The ArchiMate Access is mapped to UAF Dependency if no other ArchiMate Access relation import and mapping rule applies. |  |  |
| 2 | Aggregation | Association | Common RuleCommon Rule - after the import to UAF maps to Aggregation (Association with Aggregation = Shared) |  |
| Physical Location property | The Location information is added to the UAF Physical Location element property if the ArchiMate Aggregation relates the Actual Location to the Location Holder (i.e. Operational Performer, Resource Performer).No relation is created in UAF. |  |  |  |
| Link | The ArchiMate Aggregation is mapped to UAF Link [InstanceSpecification] if the ArchiMate Aggregation relates elements that map to the Instance Specifications with the same type. |  |  |  |
| Trace | The ArchiMate Aggregation is mapped to UAF Trace if the ArchiMate Aggregation relates ArchiMate elements (Goal, Requirement, Principle) which map to UAF Enterprise Goal or Requirement elements. |  |  |  |
| Dependency | The ArchiMate Aggregation is mapped to UAF Dependency if no other ArchiMate Aggregation relation import and mapping rule applies. |  |  |  |
| 3 | Assignment | Is Capable To Perform | The ArchiMate Assignment is mapped to UAF Is Capable To Perform if the ArchiMate Assignment relates the ArchiMate elements that map to UAF Operational Performer and Operational Activity or Resource Performer and Function. |  |
| Resource Port | The ArchiMate Assignment is mapped to UAF Resource Port when Application Interface/Technology Interface is assigned to Application Service/Technology Interface.Also, see Mapping issues. |  |  |  |
| Service Port | The ArchiMate Assignment is mapped to UAF Service Port if Business Interface is assigned to Business Service.Also, see Mapping issues. |  |  |  |
| Dependency | The ArchiMate Assignment is mapped to UAF Dependency if no other ArchiMate Assignment relation import and mapping rule applies. |  |  |  |
| 4 | Association | isDirected=true | Association | The ArchiMate Association is mapped to the UAF Association with the navigable end (the source and target are the same as in ArchiMate) |
| isDirected=False | The ArchiMate Association is mapped to the UAF Association without the navigable end (the source and target are the same as in ArchiMate) |  |  |  |
|  | Is Capable To Perform | The ArchiMate Association is mapped to the UAF Is Capable To Perform relation if ArchiMate Resource is related to Business Process. |  |  |
| Link | The ArchiMate Association is mapped to UAF Link [InstanceSpecification] if the ArchiMate Association relates elements that map to the Instance Specifications with the same type. |  |  |  |
| Actual Project Milestone Role | The ArchiMate Association is mapped to the UAF Actual Project Milestone Role [Slot] when the ArchiMAte Association relates Workpackage to Deliverable/Implementation Event. |  |  |  |
| Trace | The ArchiMate Association is mapped to the UAF Trace if the ArchiMate Association relates ArchiMate elements (Goal, Requirement, Principle) which map to UAF Enterprise Goal or Requirement elements. |  |  |  |
| Dependency | The ArchiMate Association is mapped to UAF Dependency if no other ArchiMate Association relation import and mapping rule applies. |  |  |  |
| 5 | Composition | Association | Common Rule - after import to UAF maps to Composition (Association with Aggregation = Composite) |  |
| Containment | The ArchiMate Composition is mapped to UAF Containment if the ArchiMate Composition relates ArchiMate elements (Goal, Requirement, Principle) which map to UAF Enterprise Goal or Requirement elements.Also, see Mapping issues. |  |  |  |
| Slot | If ArchiMate Composition relate elements that in UAF become Actual Measurement Sets, then in UAF Composition relation is not created, but "main" Actual Measurement Set is created as owner which owns "inner" Actual Measurement Sets (owner with slot subMeasurementType and values for this slot are created) |  |  |  |
| 6 | Connection (Line) | UAF Element onto Information → Annotated Element | The ArchiMate Connection/Line/Anchor information is added to the Annotated Element property of the UAF Information element. |  |
| 7 | Flow | Operational Exchange | The ArchiMate Flow is mapped to UAF Operational Exchange if the ArchiMate Flow relates UAF elements from the Operational Domain. |  |
| Resource Exchange | The ArchiMate Flow is mapped to UAF Resource Exchange if the ArchiMate Flow relates UAF elements from the Resource Domain. |  |  |  |
| Service Exchange | The ArchiMate Flow is mapped to UAF Service Exchange if the ArchiMate Flow relates UAF Service elements. |  |  |  |
| Operational Control Flow | The ArchiMate Flow is mapped to UAF Operational Control Flow if the ArchiMate Flow relates Business Processes that map to Operational Activity + Operational Activity Action. |  |  |  |
| Function Control Flow | The ArchiMate Flow is mapped to UAF Function Contro Flow if the ArchiMate Flow relates Technology Process, Technology Function, Application Process, Application Function, or Business Function that maps to Function + FunctionAction. |  |  |  |
| Dependency | The ArchiMate Flow is mapped to UAF Dependency if the ArchiMate Flow relation does not map to Resource Exchange or Operational Exchange. |  |  |  |
| 8 | Influence | Dependency | Common rule, but ArchiMate name and strength map to UAF Dependency name ("name strength"). |  |
| 9 | Realization | Phases | The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Phases if the ArchiMate Realization relates ArchiMate Plateau (which maps to UAF Actual Enterprise Phase) with Capability or Goal. |  |
| Exhibits | The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Exhibits if the Archimate Realization relates ArchiMate Workpackage or BusinessService with Capability. |  |  |  |
| Maps To Capability | The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Maps To Capability if the ArchiMate Realization relates ArchiMate Business Process with Capability. |  |  |  |
| Is Capable To Perform | The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Is Capable To Perform if the ArchiMate Realization relates:ArchiMate Business Function to Business Service (the source and target are opposite than in ArchiMate).ArchiMate Plateau to Business Function. |  |  |  |
| Constrained Element | The ArchiMate Realization information is added into the Constrained Element property of the UAF Constraint if the ArchiMatre Realization relates ArchiMate Constraint with any other element. No relations are created. |  |  |  |
| Desires | The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Desires if the ArchiMate Realization relates ArchiMate Capability with Outcome. |  |  |  |
| Satisfy | The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Satisfy if the ArchiMate Realization relates ArchiMate Outcome with Goal. |  |  |  |
| Composition | The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Composition if the ArchiMate Realization relates:ArchiMate Plateau (which maps to UAF Capability Configuration) to Equipment or Business Actor.ArchiMate Communication Network to Path.ArchiMate Product (which maps to UAF Service) to Business Service. |  |  |  |
| Implements | The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Implements if the ArchiMate Realization relates ArchiMate Business Actor, Equipment, Plateau to Resource or Business Role. |  |  |  |
| Version Released | The ArchiMate Realization information is added to UAF Version Released tag if the ArchiMate Realization relates ArchiMate Workpackage (which maps to UAF Actual Project and Actual Project Milestone) to Plateau (which maps to UAF Capability Configuration) or Business Service. No relationship is created. |  |  |  |
| Annotated Element | The ArchiMate Realization information is added to the UAF Annotated Element tag if the ArchiMate Realization relates ArchiMate Plateau (which maps to UAF Capability Configuration) to Grouping. No relationship is created. |  |  |  |
| Dependency | The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Dependency if no other ArchiMate Realization relation import and mapping rule applies. |  |  |  |
| 10 | Serving | Dependency | The ArchiMate Serving is mapped to UAF Dependency if no other ArchiMate Serving relation import and mapping rule applies. |  |
| Supports | The ArchiMate Serving is mapped to UAF Supports if ArchiMate Business Service is related to Business Process using the ArchiMate Serving relation (UAF Service supports Operational Activity). |  |  |  |
| 11 | Specialization | Generalization | Common rule |  |
| Containment | The ArchiMate Specialization is mapped to UAF Containment if the ArchiMate Specialization relation is between Requirements, Goals, and Principles.Also, see Mapping issues. |  |  |  |
| Dependency | The ArchiMate Specialization is mapped to UAF Dependency if no other ArchiMate Specialization relation import and mapping rule applies. |  |  |  |
| 12 | Triggering | Operational Exchange | The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Operational Exchange when the ArchiMate Triggering is related to Operational Performers. |  |
| Resource Exchange | The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Resource Exchange when the ArchiMate Triggering is related to Resources. |  |  |  |
| Service Exchange | The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Service Exchange when the Archimate Triggering is related to Services. |  |  |  |
| Operational Control Flow | The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Operational Control Flow when the ArchiMate Triggering is related to Operational Activity Actions. |  |  |  |
| Function Control Flow | The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Function Control Flow when the ArchiMate Triggering is related to Function Actions. |  |  |  |
| Service Control Flow | The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Service Control Flow when the ArchiMate Triggering is related to Service Function Actions. |  |  |  |
| Project Sequence | The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Project Sequence when the ArchiMate Triggering is related to Actual Projects. |  |  |  |
| Milestone Dependency | The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Milestone Dependency when the ArchiMate Triggering is related to Actual Project Milestones. |  |  |  |
| Transition | The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Transition when the ArchiMate Triggering is related to States. |  |  |  |
| Dependency | The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Dependency if no other ArchiMate Triggering relation import and mapping rule applies. |  |  |  |

195753002

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Element mapping' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Mapping issues' space='UAF12P2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
