ac:name="id">195753003</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="16eee05b-a6b0-465d-bd08-40f1bf328108"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">195753014</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d380ab45-a028-4d9e-8e05-b13274b2bf42"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">195753004</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target">How ArchiMate relationships are mapped to the UAF 1.2 relationships or added to UAF element properties after the import and the mapping rules are described in the following table:</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 71.3659%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 2.77644%;" /><col style="width: 11.5361%;" /><col style="width: 9.40144%;" /><col style="width: 19.994%;" /><col style="width: 56.2921%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th rowspan="2">#</th><th style="text-align: center;" colspan="2">ArchiMate 3.1 relation</th><th style="text-align: center;" rowspan="2">UAF 1.2 </th><th style="text-align: center;" rowspan="2">Mapping Rule</th></tr><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2">1</td><td rowspan="2"><span>Access</span></td><td colspan="1"><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>Read</p><p>Write</p><p>Access</p><p>Read/Write</p></td><td>Flow Property</td><td><p>The ArchiMate Access is mapped to UAF Flow Property which is applied to Interface when the ArchiMate Access relation is used to relate the ArchiMate element to Business Interface/Technology Interface/Application Interface.</p><p>Access Type is set as the direction for Flow Property:  </p><p>Read - in,</p><p>Write - out,</p><p>Access - inout,</p><p>ReadWrite - inout.</p><p>Also, see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024x/Mapping+issues">Mapping issues</a>.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><br /></td><td colspan="1">Dependency</td><td colspan="1"><p>The ArchiMate Access is mapped to UAF Dependency if no other ArchiMate Access relation import and mapping rule applies.</p></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="5">2</td><td colspan="2" rowspan="5">Aggregation<br /><br /><br /><br /></td><td>Association</td><td><p>Common Rule</p><p>Common Rule - after the import to UAF maps to Aggregation (Association with Aggregation = Shared)</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Physical Location property</td><td colspan="1"><p>The Location information is added to the UAF Physical Location element property if the ArchiMate Aggregation relates the Actual Location to the Location Holder (i.e. Operational Performer, Resource Performer).</p><p>No relation is created in UAF.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Link  </td><td><p>The ArchiMate Aggregation is mapped to UAF Link [InstanceSpecification] if the ArchiMate Aggregation relates elements that map to the Instance Specifications with the same type.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Trace</td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Aggregation is mapped to UAF Trace if the ArchiMate Aggregation relates ArchiMate elements (Goal, Requirement, Principle) which map to UAF Enterprise Goal or Requirement elements.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Dependency</td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Aggregation is mapped to UAF Dependency if no other ArchiMate Aggregation relation import and mapping rule applies.</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="4">3</td><td colspan="2" rowspan="4">Assignment<br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /></td><td>Is Capable To Perform </td><td><p>The ArchiMate Assignment is mapped to UAF Is Capable To Perform if the ArchiMate Assignment relates the ArchiMate elements that map to UAF Operational Performer and Operational Activity or Resource Performer and Function.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Resource Port </td><td colspan="1"><p>The ArchiMate Assignment is mapped to UAF Resource Port when Application Interface/Technology Interface is assigned to Application Service/Technology Interface.</p><p>Also, see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024x/Mapping+issues">Mapping issues</a>.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Service Port </td><td><p>The ArchiMate Assignment is mapped to UAF Service Port if Business Interface is assigned to Business Service.</p><p>Also, see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024x/Mapping+issues">Mapping issues</a>.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Dependency</td><td>The ArchiMate Assignment is mapped to UAF Dependency if no other ArchiMate Assignment relation import and mapping rule applies.</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="7">4</td><td rowspan="7">Association<br /><br /><br /></td><td colspan="1">isDirected=true</td><td rowspan="2">Association</td><td>The ArchiMate Association is mapped to the UAF Association <u>with</u> the navigable end (the source and target are the same as in ArchiMate)</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">isDirected=False</td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Association is mapped to the UAF Association <u>without </u>the navigable end (the source and target are the same as in ArchiMate)</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="5"><br /><br /><br /><br /></td><td>Is Capable To Perform </td><td>The ArchiMate Association is mapped to the UAF Is Capable To Perform relation if ArchiMate Resource is related to Business Process.</td></tr><tr><td>Link  </td><td><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The ArchiMate Association is mapped to UAF Link [InstanceSpecification] if the ArchiMate Association relates elements that map to the Instance Specifications with the same type.</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Actual Project Milestone Role </td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Association is mapped to the UAF Actual Project Milestone Role [Slot] when the ArchiMAte Association relates Workpackage to Deliverable/Implementation Event.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Trace</td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Association is mapped to the UAF Trace if the ArchiMate Association relates ArchiMate elements (Goal, Requirement, Principle) which map to UAF Enterprise Goal or Requirement elements.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Dependency</td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Association is mapped to UAF Dependency if no other ArchiMate Association relation import and mapping rule applies.</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="3">5</td><td colspan="2" rowspan="3">Composition<br /><br /><br /></td><td colspan="1">Association</td><td colspan="1"><p>Common Rule - after import to UAF maps to Composition (Association with Aggregation = Composite)</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Containment</span></td><td colspan="1"><p>The ArchiMate Composition is mapped to UAF Containment if the ArchiMate Composition relates ArchiMate elements (Goal, Requirement, Principle) which map to UAF Enterprise Goal or Requirement elements.</p><p>Also, see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024x/Mapping+issues">Mapping issues</a>.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Slot</td><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">If ArchiMate Composition relate elements that in UAF become Actual Measurement Sets, then in UAF Composition relation is not created, but &quot;main&quot; Actual Measurement Set is created as owner which owns &quot;inner&quot; Actual Measurement Sets (owner with slot <em>subMeasurementType</em> and values for this slot are created)</span></td></tr><tr><td>6</td><td colspan="2">Connection (Line)</td><td colspan="1">UAF Element onto Information → Annotated Element</td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Connection/Line/Anchor information is added to the Annotated Element property of the UAF Information element.</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="6">7<br /><br /><br /><br /><br /></td><td colspan="2" rowspan="6">Flow<br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /></td><td><p>Operational Exchange </p></td><td>The ArchiMate Flow is mapped to UAF Operational Exchange if the ArchiMate Flow relates UAF elements from the Operational Domain.</td></tr><tr><td>Resource Exchange </td><td>The ArchiMate Flow is mapped to UAF Resource Exchange if the ArchiMate Flow relates UAF elements from the Resource Domain.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service Exchange </td><td colspan="1"><p>The ArchiMate Flow is mapped to UAF Service Exchange if the ArchiMate Flow relates UAF Service elements.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Operational Control Flow </td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Flow is mapped to UAF Operational Control Flow if the ArchiMate Flow relates Business Processes that map to Operational Activity + Operational Activity Action.</td></tr><tr><td>Function Control Flow </td><td><p>The ArchiMate Flow is mapped to UAF Function Contro Flow if the ArchiMate Flow relates Technology Process, Technology Function, Application Process, Application Function, or Business Function that maps to Function + FunctionAction.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Dependency</p></td><td><p>The ArchiMate Flow is mapped to UAF Dependency if the ArchiMate Flow relation does not map to Resource Exchange or Operational Exchange.</p></td></tr><tr><td>8</td><td colspan="2">Influence</td><td colspan="1">Dependency</td><td colspan="1">Common rule, but ArchiMate name and strength map to UAF Dependency name (&quot;name strength&quot;).</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="12">9<br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /></td><td colspan="2" rowspan="12"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Realization</span><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /></td><td>Phases</td><td>The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Phases if the ArchiMate Realization relates ArchiMate Plateau (which maps to UAF Actual Enterprise Phase) with Capability or Goal.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Exhibits</td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Exhibits if the Archimate Realization relates ArchiMate Workpackage or BusinessService with Capability.</td></tr><tr><td>Maps To Capability</td><td>The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Maps To Capability if the ArchiMate Realization relates ArchiMate Business Process with Capability.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Is Capable To Perform</td><td colspan="1"><p>The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Is Capable To Perform if the ArchiMate Realization relates:</p><ul><li>ArchiMate Business Function to Business Service (the source and target are opposite than in ArchiMate).</li><li>ArchiMate Plateau to Business Function.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Constrained Element</span></td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Realization information is added into the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Constrained Element property of the UAF Constraint if the ArchiMatre Realization relates ArchiMate Constraint with any other element. No relations are created.</span></span></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Desires</span></td><td>The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Desires if the ArchiMate Realization relates ArchiMate Capability with Outcome.</td></tr><tr><td>Satisfy</td><td>The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Satisfy if the ArchiMate Realization relates ArchiMate Outcome with Goal.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Composition</td><td colspan="1"><p>The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Composition if the ArchiMate Realization relates:</p><ul><li>ArchiMate Plateau (which maps to UAF Capability Configuration) to Equipment or Business Actor.</li><li>ArchiMate Communication Network to Path.</li><li>ArchiMate Product (which maps to UAF Service) to Business Service.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Implements</td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Implements if the ArchiMate Realization relates ArchiMate Business Actor, Equipment, Plateau to Resource or Business Role.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Version Released</td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Realization information is added to UAF Version Released tag if the ArchiMate Realization <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">relates ArchiMate  Workpackage (which maps to UAF Actual Project and Actual Project Milestone) to Plateau (which maps to UAF Capability Configuration) or Business Service. No relationship is created.</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Annotated Element</span></td><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The ArchiMate Realization information is added to the UAF Annotated Element tag if the ArchiMate Realization</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> relates ArchiMate Plateau (which maps to UAF Capability Configuration) to Grouping. No relationship is created.</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Dependency</td><td colspan="1"><p>The ArchiMate Realization is mapped to UAF Dependency if no other ArchiMate Realization relation import and mapping rule applies.</p></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2">10</td><td colspan="2" rowspan="2">Serving <br /><br /></td><td>Dependency</td><td>The ArchiMate Serving is mapped to UAF Dependency if no other ArchiMate Serving relation import and mapping rule applies.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Supports </td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Serving is mapped to UAF Supports if ArchiMate Business Service is related to Business Process using the ArchiMate Serving relation (UAF Service supports Operational Activity).</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="3">11</td><td colspan="2" rowspan="3">Specialization</td><td>Generalization</td><td>Common rule</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Containment</td><td colspan="1"><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">The ArchiMate Specialization is mapped to UAF Containment if the ArchiMate Specialization relation is between Requirements, Goals, and Principles.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Also, see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024x/Mapping+issues">Mapping issues</a>.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Dependency</td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Specialization is mapped to UAF Dependency if no other ArchiMate Specialization relation import and mapping rule applies.</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="10">12<br /><br /><br /></td><td colspan="2" rowspan="10">Triggering<br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /></td><td>Operational Exchange </td><td>The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Operational Exchange when the ArchiMate Triggering is related to Operational Performers.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Resource Exchange </td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Resource Exchange when the ArchiMate Triggering is related to Resources.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service Exchange </td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Service Exchange when the Archimate Triggering is related to Services.</td></tr><tr><td>Operational Control Flow </td><td>The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Operational Control Flow when the ArchiMate Triggering is related to Operational Activity Actions.</td></tr><tr><td>Function Control Flow </td><td>The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Function Control Flow when the ArchiMate Triggering is related to Function Actions.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service Control Flow </td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Service Control Flow when the ArchiMate Triggering is related to Service Function Actions.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Project Sequence </td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Project Sequence when the ArchiMate Triggering is related to Actual Projects.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Milestone Dependency </td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Milestone Dependency when the ArchiMate Triggering is related to Actual Project Milestones.</td></tr><tr><td>Transition</td><td>The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Transition when the ArchiMate Triggering is related to States.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Dependency</td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Triggering is mapped to UAF Dependency if no other ArchiMate Triggering relation import and mapping rule applies.</td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d4f4222a-38a0-4bba-8036-6487dce2b7c8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">195753002</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723500 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00872: Removed elements

- page_id: `136723500`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723500/Removed+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:18.414+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.1 > Model migration > Elements mapping
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

All removed elements are no longer used in UAF 1.1 and don't map to the other elements.

If the element property or relationship does not exists in UAF 1.1, the additional information is added into the migrated element's To Do property. For more information about how to use To Do property, see in the page [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using To Do property' space='UAF12P2024x'].

| UPDM 2.1 |
| --- |
| Activity Part Of Project |
| Activity Subject |
| Asynchronous Message |
| Association Of Information |
| Competence Provider |
| Competence Requirer |
| Condition Kind |
| Condition Property |
| Condition Type |
| Contract |
| Design Rule |
| Desired State |
| Details |
| Entity Attribute |
| Entity Item |
| Entity Relationship |
| External Individual |
| External Type |
| External Tuple |
| External Tuple Type |
| Geo Political Extent |
| Known Resource |
| Mission |
| Ontology Reference |
| Operational Event Trace |
| Operational State |
| Overlap |
| Participant |
| Process |
| Project |
| Resource Event Trace |
| Resource State |
| Semantic |
| Semantic Attribute |
| Service Description |
| Service Feature |
| Service Function Edge |
| Service Interaction |
| Service Level Value |
| Service Message Handler |
| Standard Configuration |
| Stereotype Extension |
| Subject Of Operational State Machine |
| Transactional |
| Transactional Attribute |
| View |
| Viewpoint |
| Wrapper |
| Wrapper Attribute |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721383 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00873: Removing actual project status in NPV-1 diagram

- page_id: `136721383`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721383/Removing+actual+project+status+in+NPV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:05.047+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF > NATO Programme Viewpoint > NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationships
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To remove the project status

1. From the milestone shortcut menu, select Edit Status .
2. In the opened Project Status Application Wizard , click the Remove project status indicator mark button.
3. Click Finish when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To remove the project status</p><hr /><ol><li>From the milestone shortcut menu, select <strong>Edit Status</strong>.</li><li>In the opened <strong>Project Status Application Wizard</strong>, click the Remove project status indicator mark button.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721343 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00874: Removing capabilities from NCV-3 chart

- page_id: `136721343`

- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint > NCV-3 Capability Phasing
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To remove a capability from the chart

1. Select a capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Remove From Chart .

To delete a capability from the model

1. Select a capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Delete

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To remove a capability from the chart</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a capability.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Remove From Chart</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To delete a capability from the model</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a capability.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Delete</strong></li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721612 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->

- page_id: `136721612`

- labels: ['remove-project-status-from-lr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To remove the Project Status

1. Right-click the Milestone and select Edit Status . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Status Application Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'] opens (step #2).
2. In the right bottom corner of the Preview area , click [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_project_status_indicating_mark.png'] .
3. Click Finish when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To remove the Project Status</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Milestone and select <strong>Edit Status</strong>. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Project Status Application Wizard" /></ac:link> opens (step #2).</li><li>In the right bottom corner of the Preview area , click <ac:image ac:alt="Remove project status indicating mark" ac:title="Remove project status indicating mark"><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_project_status_indicating_mark.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

````

- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`

- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:13.553+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UAF projects migration manual > Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.1
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following table describes element which are renamed in UAF 1.1 profile.

| Element UAF 1.0 [name] | Element UAF 1.1 [name] |
| --- | --- |
| CapabilityProperty | CapabilityRole |
| SecurityProperty* | InformationRole |

* For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Mapping solutions and issues' space='UAF12P2024x'].

The following table describes properties which are renamed in UAF 1.1 profile.

| Element | Property UAF 1.0 [name] | Property UAF 1.1 [name] |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Concern | systemConcern | enterprisePhase |
| Viewpoint | languagesmethods | languagemethod |
| OperationalExchange | trustlevel | trustLevel |
| ResponsibleFor | ResponsibleRoleKind | responsibleRoleKind |
| Architecture | desribedBy | describedBy |
| ArchitecturalDescription | viewPoint | viewpoint |

**Removed:**

- Condition::conditionKind ta g was removed. For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Mapping solutions and issues' space='UAF12P2024x'] .

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723499 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00884: Replaced and renamed elements

- page_id: `136723499`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723499/Replaced+and+renamed+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:18.289+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.1 > Model migration > Elements mapping
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The replaced elements mean that their names and/or metaclasses are changed. Pay attention, that some elements from UPDM 2.1 are replaced to one element in UAF 1.1.

| UPDM 2.1 | UAF 1.1 | Comments |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Activity Part Of Capability [Dependency] | Maps To Capability [Abstraction] | Maps To Capability relationship denotes that an Activity contributes to providing a Capability. |
| Activity Performed By Performer [Dependency]Is Capable Of Performing [Dependency] | Is Capable To Perform [Abstraction] | Is Capable To Perform relationship defines the traceability between the Capable Elements to the Activities that they can perform. |
| Actual Organization Relationship | Actual Resource Relationship | Actual Resource Relationship is an abstract element that details the Actual Organizational Resources that are able to carry out an Actual Responsibility. |
| Actual Property | Actual Measurement | Actual Measurement is an actual value that is applied to a Measurement. |
| Capability Of Performer [Dependency] | Exhibits [Abstraction] | Exhibits relationship that exists between a capable element and a Capability that it meets under specific environmental conditions. |
| Capability Property | Capability Role |  |
| ClimateLight Condition | Environment | A definition of the environmental factors in which something exists or functions. The definition of an Environment element can be further defined using EnvironmentKind. |
| Deployed MilestoneNo Longer Used MilestoneOut Of Service MilestoneIncrement MilestoneActual Project Milestone | Actual Project Milestone | Actual Project Milestone is an event with a start date in an Actual Project from which progress is measured. Actual Project Milestone can have such kinds:In Service.Deployed.No Longer Used.Out Of Service.Other. |
| EnergyMateriel | Natural Resource | Natural Resource is any type of physical resource that occurs in nature such as oil, water, gas, or coal. |
| Exchange Element | Data ElementInformation Element | If the Exchange Element was conveyed by Resource Interaction it is migrated to Data Element. The Data Element is formalized representation of data that is managed by or exchanged between systems.If the Exchange Element was conveyed by Operational Exchange or wasn't conveyed, it is migrated to Information Element. The Information Element is an item of information that flows between Operational Performers and is produced and consumed by the Operational Activities that the Operational Performers are capable to perform (see Is Capable To Perform). |
| Functional Standard | Standard | A ratified and peer-reviewed specification that is used to guide or constrain the architecture. A Standard may be applied to any element in the architecture. |
| Function Edge [Activity Edge] | Functional Control Flow [Control Flow]Functional Object Flow [Object Flow] | Function Edge is an abstract grouping for Function Control Flow and Function Object Flow.Function Object Flow connects Function Actions using Pins.Function Control Flow connects Function Actions directly. |
| Project | Actual Project | Actual Project defines a time-limited endeavor to provide a specific set of Actual Resources that meet specific Capability needs. |
| Individual Person Role | Actual Post | An actual, specific post, is an instance of a Post "type" - e.g., "President of the United States of America" where the Post would be president. |
| Location | Actual Location | Actual Location is an Actual State that describes a physical location, for example using text to provide an address, Geo-coordinates, etc. |
| Location Type | Location | Location element denote a specification of the generic area in which a location holder is required to be located. |
| Logical Architecture | Operational Architecture | Operational Architecture is an element used to denote a model of the architecture, described from the business operational perspective. |
| Measure | Actual Measurement Set | Actual Measurement Set denote a set of Actual Measurements. |
| Measure TypeSecurity Attributes Group | Measurement Set | Measurement Set denote a set of Measurements. |
| Milestone Sequence | Milestone Dependency | Milestone Dependency relationship is relationship between two Actual Project Milestones that denotes one Actual Project Milestone follows from another. |
| Needline | Operational Connector | Operational Connector is a Connector that goes between Operational Roles representing a need to exchange resources. It can carry a number of Operational Exchanges. |
| Node Operation | Operational Method | Operational Method is a behavioral feature of a Operational Performer whose behavior is specified in an Operational Activity. |
| Node Parent | Operational Agent | An abstract element grouping Operational Architecture and Operational Performer. |
| Node Port | Operational Port | Operational Port is used of a Operational Performer or Operational Architecture in the context of another Operational Performer or Operational Architecture. The whole-part relationship is created. |
| Node Role | Operational Role | Usage of a Operational Performer or Operational Architecture in the context of another Operational Performer or Operational Architecture. Creates a whole-part relationship. |
| Operational Activity Edge [Activity Edge] | Operational Control Flow [Control Flow]Operational Object Flow [Object Flow] | Operational Activity Edge is an abstract grouping for Operational Control Flow and Operational Object Flow.Operational Object Flow connects Operational Activity Actions using Pins.Operational Control Flow connects Operational Activity Actions directly. |
| Organizational Project RelationshipProject Ownership | Responsible For | Responsible For relationship is relationship between an Actual Responsible Resource and an Actual Responsibility or Actual Project. It defines the duties that the Actual Responsible Resource is Responsible For. |
| Organization Type | Organization | Organization element denote a group of organizational resources (Persons, Posts, Organizations and Responsibilities) associated for a particular purpose. |
| NodePerformerSecurity Domain | Operational Performer | Operational Performer denote a logical entity that Is Capable To Perform Operational Activities which produce, consume and process resources. |
| Person Type | Post | Post denote a type of job title or position that a person can fill (e.g. Lawyer, Solution Architect, Machine Operator or Chief Executive Officer). |
| Physical Architecture | Resource Architecture | Resource Architecture is an element used to denote a model of the Architecture, described from the System Resource perspective. |
| Project Type | Project | Project is an element that describes types of time-limited endeavours that are required to meet one or more Capability needs. |
| Property | Measurement | Measurement is a property of an element representing something in the physical world, expressed in amounts of a unit of measure. |
| RequestService | Operational PortResource Port | Operational Port is used of a Operational Performer or Operational Architecture in the context of another Operational Performer or Operational Architecture. The whole-part relationship is created.If Request port belongs to System Resource it is migrated to Resource Port.If Request port belongs to Operational Performer it is migrated to Operational Port.Resource Port is an interaction point for a System Resource through which it can interact with the outside environment and which is defined by a Resource Connector. If Service port belongs to System Resource it is migrated to Resource Port.If Service port belongs to Operational Performer it is migrated to Operational Port. |
| Resource Interaction | Resource Exchange |  |
| Resource Interaction Item | Resource Exchange Item | Resource Exchange Item is an abstract grouping for elements that defines the types of elements that can be exchanged between System Resources and conveyed by a Resource Exchange. |
| Resource Interface | Resource Connector | Resource Connector is a channel for exchange between two Resource Roles. |
| Resource State Machine | Resource State Description | A state machine describing the behavior of a System Resource, depicting how the System Resource responds to various events and the actions. |
| Resource Operation | Resource Method |  |
| Role Type | Responsibility | Responsibility element is the type of duty required of a Person or Organization. |
| Service Access | Capability ConfigurationService Specification | Capability Configuration represents a composite structure the physical and human resources (and their interactions) in an enterprise, assembled to meet a Capability.Service Specification defines the specification of a set of functionality provided by one element for the use of others. |
| Service Attribute | Flow Property | Flow Property is a SysML element with already defined direction that is inout. A Flow Property signifies a single flow element that can flow to/from a Block. A flow property’s values are either received from or transmitted to an external Block. Flow properties are defined directly on Blocks or Flow Specifications that are those specifications which type the Flow Ports. Flow properties enable item flows across connectors connecting parts of the corresponding block types, either directly (in case of the property is defined on the Block) or via Flow Ports. |
| Service Channel | Resource ConnectorOperational Connector | If Service Channel connects Ports which owner is System Resource it is be migrated to Resource Connector.If Service Channel connects Ports which owner is Operational Performer it is be migrated to Operational Connector. |
| Service Level Value Set | Provided Service Level | Provided Service Level is a sub type of Actual Service that details a specific service level delivered by the provider. |
| Service State Machine | Service State Description | Service State Description is a state machine describing the behavior of a Service Specification, depicting how the Service Specification responds to various events and the actions. |
| Skill | Competence | Competence element is a specific set of abilities defined by knowledge, skills and aptitude. |
| Skill Of Person Type | Provides Competence | Provides Competence is a dependency relationship that asserts that an Actual Organizational Resource provides a specific set of Competencies. |
| System | Resource Artifact | Resource Artifact element is a type of man-made object that contains no human beings (i.e. satellite, radio, petrol, gasoline, etc.). |
| System Resource | Resource Performer | The Resource Performer covers the Resource Architecture, , Known Resource.Physical Resource |
| Service Operation | Service Method | A behavioral feature of a Service Specification whose behavior is specified in a Service Function. |
| Technical Standard | Standard | Standard is a ratified and peer-reviewed specification that is used to guide or constrain the architecture. A Standard may be applied to any element in the architecture. |
| Trustline | Operational Exchange | Operational Exchange asserts that a flow can exist between Operational Performers (i.e. flows of information, people, materiel, or energy). |
| Vision | Enterprise Vision | An Enterprise Vision describes the future state of the enterprise, without regard to how it is to be achieved. |
| Activity Part Of Project [Dependency]Overlap [Dependency] | Dependency | The Dependency relationship is used instead of stereotyped dependencies such as Activity Part Of Project and Overlap. |
| Association Of Information [Association]Entity Relationship [Association] | Association | The Association relationship is used instead of stereotyped associations such as Association Of Information and Entity Relationship. |
| Asynchronous Message [Signal] | Signal | The Signal element is used instead of stereotyped signal element Asynchronous Message. |
| Condition Property [Property]Entity Attribute [Property] | Property | The Property is used instead of stereotyped properties such as Condition Property and Entity Attribute. |
| Design Rule [Constraint] | Constraint | The Constraint is used instead of stereotyped constraint Design Rule. |
| Geo Political Extent [Instance Specification] | Instance Specification | The Instance Specification is used instead of stereotyped instance specification Geo Political Extent . |
| Logical Data Model [Data Model]Physical Data Model [Data Model] | Data Model | The Data Model is used instead of stereotyped data models such as Logical Data Model and Physical Data Model. |
| Operational Event Trace [Interaction]Resource Event Trace [Interaction]Service Interaction [Interaction] | Interaction | The Interaction is used instead of stereotyped interactions such as Operational Event Trace, Resource Event Trace, and Service Interaction. |
| Operational State [State]Resource State [State] | State | The State is used instead of stereotyped states such as Operational State and Resource State. |
| Project Activity Edge [Activity Edge] | Activity Edge | The Activity Edge is used instead of stereotyped activity edge Project Activity Edge. |
| Service Description [Package] | Package | The Package is used instead of stereotyped package Service Description. |
| Service Level Value [Slot] | Slot | The Slot is used instead of stereotyped slot Service Level Value. |
| Service Message Handler [Reception] | Reception | The Reception is used instead of stereotyped reception Service Message Handler. |
| UPDM Element | UAF Element |  |

The following table describes properties which are renamed in UAF 1.1 profile.

| Element | Property UPDM 2.1 [name] | Property UAF 1.1 [name] |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Viewpoint | languagesmethods | languagemethod |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The replaced elements mean that their names and/or metaclasses are changed. Pay attention, that some elements from UPDM 2.1 are replaced to one element in <span>UAF 1</span>.1.</p><table class="relative-table" style="width: 94.5694%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 18.3981%;" /><col style="width: 16.8891%;" /><col style="width: 64.7127%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>UPDM 2.1</th><th><span>UAF 1</span>.1</th><th>Comments</th></tr><tr><td>Activity Part Of Capability [Dependency]</td><td>Maps To Capability [Abstraction]</td><td><p>Maps To Capability relationship denotes that an Activity contributes to providing a Capability.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Activity Performed By Performer [Dependency]</p><p>Is Capable Of Performing [Dependency]</p></td><td colspan="1">Is Capable To Perform [Abstraction]</td><td colspan="1">Is Capable To Perform relationship defines the traceability between the Capable Elements to the Activities that they can perform.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Actual Organization Relationship</td><td colspan="1">Actual Resource Relationship</td><td colspan="1">Actual Resource Relationship is an abstract element that details the Actual Organizational Resources that are able to carry out an Actual Responsibility.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Actual Property</td><td colspan="1">Actual Measurement</td><td colspan="1">Actual Measurement is an actual value that is applied to a Measurement.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Capability Of Performer [Dependency]</td><td colspan="1">Exhibits [Abstraction]</td><td colspan="1">Exhibits relationship that exists between a capable element and a Capability that it meets under specific environmental conditions.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Capability Property</span></td><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Capability Role</span></td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Climate</p><p>Light Condition</p></td><td colspan="1">Environment</td><td colspan="1">A definition of the environmental factors in which something exists or functions. The definition of an Environment element can be further defined using EnvironmentKind.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="69cd5b13-b74b-44aa-a5b6-3461f70bcf08">Deployed Milestone</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p>No Longer Used Milestone</p><p>Out Of Service Milestone</p><p>Increment Milestone</p><p><span>Actual Project Milestone</span></p></td><td colspan="1">Actual Project Milestone</td><td colspan="1"><p>Actual Project Milestone is an event with a start date in an Actual Project from which progress is measured. Actual Project Milestone can have such kinds:</p><ul><li>In Service.</li><li>Deployed.</li><li>No Longer Used.</li><li>Out Of Service.</li><li>Other.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Energy</p><p>Materiel</p></td><td colspan="1">Natural Resource</td><td colspan="1">Natural Resource is any type of physical resource that occurs in nature such as oil, water, gas, or coal.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Exchange Element</p><p> </p></td><td colspan="1"><p>Data Element</p><p>Information Element</p></td><td colspan="1"><p>If the Exchange Element was conveyed by Resource Interaction it is migrated to Data Element. The Data Element is formalized representation of data that is managed by or exchanged between systems.</p><p>If the Exchange Element was conveyed by Operational Exchange or wasn't conveyed, it is migrated to Information Element. The Information Element is an item of information that flows between Operational Performers and is produced and consumed by the Operational Activities that the Operational Performers are capable to perform (see Is Capable To Perform).</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Functional Standard</td><td colspan="1">Standard</td><td colspan="1">A ratified and peer-reviewed specification that is used to guide or constrain the architecture. A Standard may be applied to any element in the architecture.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Function Edge [Activity Edge]</p></td><td colspan="1"><p>Functional Control Flow [Control Flow]</p><p>Functional Object Flow [Object Flow]</p></td><td colspan="1"><p>Function Edge is an abstract grouping for Function Control Flow and Function Object Flow.</p><p>Function Object Flow connects Function Actions using Pins.</p><p>Function Control Flow connects Function Actions directly.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="54713ff5-5455-4d6b-8b14-1cd1b27e3f79"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d4857158-626d-475e-a994-72c2005a6a32">Project</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></p></td><td colspan="1"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8d3ddea5-b2ec-441b-a14c-3b51d3cf57c0">Actual Project</ac:inline-comment-marker></td><td colspan="1">Actual Project defines a time-limited endeavor to provide a specific set of Actual Resources that meet specific Capability needs.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Individual Person Role</td><td colspan="1">Actual Post</td><td colspan="1">An actual, specific post, is an instance of a Post &quot;type&quot; - e.g., &quot;President of the United States of America&quot; where the Post would be president.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Location</td><td colspan="1">Actual Location</td><td colspan="1">Actual Location is an Actual State that describes a physical location, for example using text to provide an address, Geo-coordinates, etc.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Location Type</td><td colspan="1">Location</td><td colspan="1">Location element denote a specification of the generic area in which a location holder is required to be located.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Logical Architecture</td><td colspan="1">Operational Architecture</td><td colspan="1">Operational Architecture is an element used to denote a model of the architecture, described from the business operational perspective.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Measure</td><td colspan="1">Actual Measurement Set</td><td colspan="1">Actual Measurement Set denote a set of Actual Measurements.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Measure Type</p><p>Security Attributes Group</p></td><td colspan="1">Measurement Set</td><td colspan="1">Measurement Set denote a set of Measurements.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Milestone Sequence</td><td colspan="1">Milestone Dependency</td><td colspan="1">Milestone Dependency relationship is relationship between two Actual Project Milestones that denotes one Actual Project Milestone follows from another.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Needline</td><td colspan="1">Operational Connector</td><td colspan="1">Operational Connector is a Connector that goes between Operational Roles representing a need to exchange resources. It can carry a number of Operational Exchanges.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Node Operation</span></td><td colspan="1">Operational Method</td><td colspan="1">Operational Method is a behavioral feature of a Operational Performer whose behavior is specified in an Operational Activity.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Node Parent</span></td><td colspan="1">Operational Agent</td><td colspan="1">An abstract element grouping Operational Architecture and Operational Performer.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Node Port</span></td><td colspan="1">Operational Port</td><td colspan="1">Operational Port is used of a Operational Performer or Operational Architecture in the context of another Operational Performer or Operational Architecture. The whole-part relationship is created.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Node Role</span></td><td colspan="1">Operational Role</td><td colspan="1">Usage of a Operational Performer or Operational Architecture in the context of another Operational Performer or Operational Architecture. Creates a whole-part relationship.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Operational Activity Edge [Activity Edge]</td><td colspan="1"><p>Operational Control Flow [Control Flow]</p><p>Operational Object Flow [Object Flow]</p></td><td colspan="1"><p>Operational Activity Edge is an abstract grouping for Operational Control Flow and Operational Object Flow.</p><p>Operational Object Flow connects Operational Activity Actions using Pins.</p><p>Operational Control Flow connects Operational Activity Actions directly.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Organizational Project Relationship</p><p>Project Ownership</p></td><td colspan="1">Responsible For</td><td colspan="1"><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Responsible For relationship is relationship between an Actual Responsible Resource and an Actual Responsibility or Actual Project. **Description**

A sub type of Actual Service that details a specific service level required of the provider.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF

**Extension**

UML Instance Specification

## PAGE 00887: Requirements

- page_id: `136722503`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722503/Requirements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:18.674+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

914119321

914119323

914119322

**Description**

The **Requirements Domain** (Rq) represent requirements, their properties, and relationships (trace, verify, satisfy, refine) between each other and to UAF architectural elements.

**Implementation**

A Requirements viewpoint can be represented using:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Requirement Table' space='SYSMLP2024x'] .

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722598 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00893: Resource Assignment Wizard

- page_id: `136722598`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722598/Resource+Assignment+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:24.603+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Resource Assignment Wizard** is used to define resource for the Version Of Configuration in specified time frame.

To open the **Resource Assignment Wizard**

1. Open a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-8 Systems Evolution Description' space='UAF12P2024x'] (DoDAF), SV-8 (MODAF), NSV-8 (NAF), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Pr Configuration Management' space='UAF12P2024x'] (NAF 4.0), or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Evolution diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] (UAF)
2. On the diagram pane, right-click the Version Of Configuration and select Resource . The Resource Assignment Wizard opens.

The wizard consists of three steps:

truenone

##### Resource selection

In this step, you can select the existing or create newresource/ActualResource that will be assigned for Version Of Configuration.

If you select or create new resource, the corresponding Actual Resource will be created automatically.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create a resource for the Version Of Configuration

1. In the Select Resource dialog switch on the Creation Mode .
2. Select package where you want to create a resource.
3. Click the Create button, choose a resource category Security , Resources , or Business , and select the desired resource.
4. In the Specification window of the newly created resource, type its name.

##### Start date specification

In this step, you can set the start date (or select the date from the Timeline) when selected Actual Resource is being used in Version Of Configuration.

The Actual Project Milestone with Kind InService is created. Resource is assigned to the Actual Project Milestone.

For more information on setting the day and time, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UAF12P2024x']**Date and Time Settings** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### End date specification

In this step, you can set the end date (or select the date from the Timeline) when selected Actual Resource is being used in Version Of Configuration.

The Actual Project Milestone with Kind OutOfService is created. Resource is assigned to the Actual Project Milestone.

For more information on setting the day and time, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UAF12P2024x']**Date and Time Settings** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

select <strong>Resource</strong>.<br />The <strong>Resource Assignment Wizard</strong> opens.</li></ol><p>The wizard consists of three steps:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723339 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00898: Resource Exchange Kind

- page_id: `136723339`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723339/Resource+Exchange+Kind
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:07.097+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Enumeration of the possible kinds of resource exchange applicable to a ResourceExchange. Its enumeration literals are:

- ResourceCommunication - Indicates that the ResourceExchange associated with the ResourceExchangeKind is an implementation of the logical flow of data between Resources.
- ResourceMovement - Indicates that the ResourceExchange associated with the ResourceExchangeKind is an implementation of the logical flow of Resources between Resources.
- ResourceEnergyFlow - Indicates that the ResourceExchange associated with the ResourceExchangeKind is an implementation of the logical flow of natural resources between Resources.
- GeoPoliticalExtentExchange - Indicates that the ResourceExchange associated with the ResourceExchangeKind is an implementation of logical flow where GeoPoliticalExtents (i.e., Borders) flow from one place to another.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGBoldBulletKeepNextParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">Enumeration of the possible kinds of resource exchange applicable to a ResourceExchange. Its enumeration literals are:</p><ul><li>ResourceCommunication - Indicates that the<span> </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">ResourceExchange<span> </span></span>associated with the<span> </span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">ResourceExchangeKind<span> </span></span>is an implementation of the logical flow of data between Resources.</li><li>ResourceMovement - Indicates that the<span> </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">ResourceExchange<span> </span></span>associated with the<span> </span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">ResourceExchangeKind<span> </span></span>is an implementation of the logical flow of Resources between Resources.</li><li>ResourceEnergyFlow - Indicates that the<span> </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">ResourceExchange<span> </span></span>associated with the<span> </span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">ResourceExchangeKind<span> </span></span>is an implementation of the logical flow of natural resources between Resources.</li><li>GeoPoliticalExtentExchange - Indicates that the<span> </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">ResourceExchange<span> </span></span>associated with the<span> </span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">ResourceExchangeKind<span> </span></span>is an implementation of logical flow where GeoPoliticalExtents (i.e., Borders) flow from one place to another.</li></ul><p><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p>UAF</p>
````

1014834718

1014834720

1014834719

The **Resource Exchange Manager** dialog enables the analysis and management of the Resource Exchange data. You can:

- Edit existing Resource Exchanges by adding new Resource Exchange Items
- Realize or ignore existing Resource Exchanges on selected relationship
- Change the Resource Exchange Item [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_resource_exchange_item.png']
- Review producing and consuming Function pairs and remove the ones that are not needed
- Review a list of diagrams that display the Realizing Elements of the selected Exchange

To open the **Resource Exchange Manager** dialog

1. Select Resource Connector, Association,Function Control Flow , Function Object Flow, or Resource Message in the diagram pane.
2. On the smart manipulator, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange_manager.png'] . The Resource Exchange Manager dialog opens.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1014834716

## PAGE 00902: Resource Interface

- page_id: `136723342`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723342/Resource+Interface
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:07.246+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Description

A declaration that specifies a contract between the Resource Performers it is related to and any other Resource Performers it can interact with. It is also intended to be an implementation of a specification of an Interface in the Business and/or Service layer.

Architecture Framework

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF, UAF

Extension

UML Class

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p class="OMGBoldBulletKeepNextParagraph">Description</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">A declaration that specifies a contract between the Resource Performers it is related to and any other Resource Performers it can interact with. It is also intended to be an implementation of a specification of an Interface in the Business and/or Service layer. </p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">Architecture Framework</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span class="fontstyle0">DoDAF, MODAF, NAF,</span> UAF</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">Extension</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UML Class</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00909: Resource Role

- page_id: `136723349`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723349/Resource+Role
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:07.613+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

728086762

728086766

728086764

**Description**

Usage of a Resource Performer in the context of another Resource Performer. Creates a whole-part relationship.

**Architecture Framework**

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF, UAF

**Extension**

UML Property

728086761

###### **Resource Role Kind mapping table by OMG specification**

For each Resource Role created under the Resource Performer (which is a context), a type (which is Resource Performer) should be assigned. Depending on a type, an appropriate Role Kind is assigned for each Resource Role according to the rules provided in the following table.

| Role Kind | Type | Context |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Artifact Component | Resource Artifact | Resource Performer |
| Component | Software | Resource Performer |
| Equipment | Physical Resource | Resource Performer |
| Hosted Software | Software | Resource Performer |
| Human Resource | Organizational Resource | Resource Performer |
| Natural Resource Component | Natural Resource | Resource Performer |
| Other | Resource Performer | Resource Performer |
| Part | Resource Performer | Resource Performer |
| Platform | Resource Performer | Resource Performer |
| Post Role | Post | Resource Performer |
| Responsibility Role | Responsibility | Resource Performer |
| System | Resource Performer | Resource Performer |
| Sub Organization | Organization | Resource Performer |
| Sub System Part | Resource Performers | Resource Performer |
| Used Configuration | Capability Configuration | Resource Performer |
| Used Physical Architecture | Resource Architecture | Resource Performer |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ef8ad4f-7d39-4572-aab5-2875490f113d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">728086762</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4f57d44c-6389-4e00-979a-b6613c2af0e7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">728086766</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="728db62f-ec92-4198-a70a-886f6f43a2af"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">728086764</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="OMGBoldBulletKeepNextParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">Usage of a Resource Performer in the context of another Resource Performer. Creates a whole-part relationship.</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span class="fontstyle0">DoDAF, MODAF, NAF, UAF</span></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UML Property</p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d8b5a443-4b88-4085-8ce0-d4dad2adb50a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">728086761</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h4><strong>Resource Role Kind mapping table by OMG specification</strong></h4><p>For each Resource Role created under the Resource Performer (which is a context), a type (which is Resource Performer) should be assigned. Depending on a type, an appropriate Role Kind is assigned for each Resource Role according to the rules provided in the following table.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 44.0761%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 33.5802%;" /><col style="width: 35.1852%;" /><col style="width: 31.2346%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Role Kind</th><th>Type</th><th>Context</th></tr><tr><td>Artifact Component </td><td>Resource Artifact</td><td>Resource Performer</td></tr><tr><td>Component </td><td>Software </td><td>Resource Performer </td></tr><tr><td>Equipment </td><td>Physical Resource </td><td>Resource Performer</td></tr><tr><td>Hosted Software </td><td>Software </td><td>Resource Performer</td></tr><tr><td>Human Resource</td><td>Organizational Resource</td><td>Resource Performer </td></tr><tr><td>Natural Resource Component </td><td>Natural Resource</td><td>Resource Performer</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Other</td><td colspan="1">Resource Performer </td><td colspan="1">Resource Performer </td></tr><tr><td>Part </td><td>Resource Performer </td><td>Resource Performer </td></tr><tr><td>Platform </td><td>Resource Performer </td><td>Resource Performer </td></tr><tr><td>Post Role</td><td>Post</td><td>Resource Performer</td></tr><tr><td>Responsibility Role</td><td>Responsibility </td><td>Resource Performer</td></tr><tr><td>System </td><td>Resource Performer </td><td>Resource Performer </td></tr><tr><td>Sub Organization</td><td>Organization </td><td>Resource Performer </td></tr><tr><td>Sub System Part </td><td>Resource Performers</td><td>Resource Performer</td></tr><tr><td>Used Configuration </td><td>Capability Configuration</td><td>Resource Performer </td></tr><tr><td>Used Physical Architecture </td><td>Resource Architecture </td><td>Resource Performer</td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A services that a Resource Performer provides to support higher level Services or Operational Activities. Employee provisioning, backup and recovery, storage, self-service help desk are examples of Resource Services.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF

**Extensions**

Class

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">A services that a Resource Performer provides to support higher level Services or Operational Activities. Employee provisioning, backup and recovery, storage, self-service help desk are examples of Resource Services.</p><p><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p>UAF</p><p><strong>Extensions</strong></p><p>Class</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723352 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00912: Resource Signal

- page_id: `136723352`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723352/Resource+Signal
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:07.799+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A ResourceSignal is a specification of a kind of communication between resources (Resource Performers) in which a reaction is asynchronously triggered in the receiver without a reply.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF

**Extension**

Signal

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description </strong></p><p>A ResourceSignal is a specification of a kind of communication between resources (Resource Performers) in which a reaction is asynchronously triggered in the receiver without a reply.</p><p><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p>UAF</p><p><strong>Extension</strong></p><p>Signal</p>
````

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

914204988

914204990

914204989

The Resources (Rs) domain defines the solution architectures to implement operational requirements. It captures a solution architecture consisting of resources, e.g. organizational, software, artifacts, capability configurations, natural resources.

914204987

**Views**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722191 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00915: Resources Connectivity

- page_id: `136722191`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722191/Resources+Connectivity
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:15:40.563+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

914248742

914248744

914248743

**Description**

The Resources Connectivity (Rs-Cn) table summarizes the exchanges between resources of information, systems, personnel, natural resources, etc., and the functions that produce and consume them. Measurements can optionally be included.

The Resources Connectivity Internal diagram addresses the internal composition and interaction of systems.

The intended usage of the The Resources Connectivity (Rs-Cn) view includes:

- Summarizing system resource exchanges.
- Interface management.
- Comparing interoperability characteristics of solution options.
- Detailed definition of resource flows.

**Implementation**

The Resources Connectivity (Rs-Cn) is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Internal Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Connectivity matrix' space='UAF12P2024x'] . It represents exchanges between resources.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Role-based Connectivity Table' space='UAF12P2024x'] . It represents exchanges between Resource Roles (resource usages).
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Connectivity Table' space='UAF12P2024x']
- Reports (.xlsx): - Resources Connectivity Table Excel Spreadsheet. - Resources Role-based Connectivity Table Excel Spreadsheet.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### An example of the Resources Internal Connectivity diagram

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Resources Connectivity Table

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the resources connectivity matrix

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Resources Role-based Connectivity Table

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722211 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00916: Resources Constraints

- page_id: `136722211`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722211/Resources+Constraints
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:43:14.153+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

914505518

914505520

914505519

**Description**

The Resources Constraints (Rs-Ct) domain defines limitations, constraints, and performance parameters for resources, their interactions, performed functions, and data. It specifies traditional textual rules/non-functional requirements. The addition of SysML parametrics provide a computational means of defining resource constraints within a specific context.

The intended usage of the Resources Constraints (Rs-Ct) view includes:

- Definition of implementation logic.
- Identification of resource constraints.

**Implementation**

The Resources Constraints (Rs-Ct) view is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Constraints table' space='UAF12P2024x'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Constraints Definition diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] .
- Resources Constraints Excel Spreadsheet (.xlsx) report.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Resources Constraints table

**Constraint owner**

You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the **Project Options**.

To select a constraint owner

1. In the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Go to General > UAF , and in the Default Constraint Owners property group, choose a needed owner.

914505517

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722606 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00917: Resources Forecast Creation Wizard

- page_id: `136722606`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722606/Resources+Forecast+Creation+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:25.097+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1622433108

1622433119

1622433109

The **Resources Forecast Creation Wizard** is used to create forecast tables.

The same wizard is used for the following tables:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating the SV-9 table' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-9 table' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating StdV-2 table' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating NSV-9 table' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating NTV-2 table' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating the SV-9 table' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating TV-2 table' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P1 Technology Forecast table' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating A8 Standards Forecast table' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Standards Roadmap table' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Forecast table' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Forecast diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

To open the **Resources Forecast Creation Wizard**

- Do one of the following:
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram. The Resources Forecast Creation Wizard opens.

The wizard consists of three steps:

truenone

##### Diagram name and owning package specification

In this step, you can select the existing or create a new owner for the created diagram. You can also type the diagram name.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Resources selection

In this step, choose the needed resources or create new resources. The selected resources will be represented as Resources Forecast table rows.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The resources (Capability Configuration, Competence, Known Resource, Natural Resource, Organization, Person, Post, Project, Protocol, protocol Stack, Resource Architecture, Resource Artifact, Resource Mitigation, Responsibility, Security Enclave, Software, Standard, System, Technology) are offered depending on the domain.

To create a resource

1. In the Select Resources step, click the Creation Mode .
2. Select the package where you want to create a resource.
3. Click the Create button.
4. From the list, select the resource type.
5. In the Specification window of the newly created resource, type its name.

##### Actual Enterprise Phases selection

In this step, you can select existing or create new Actual Enterprise Phases which will be represented as Resources Forecast table columns.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create an Actual Enterprise Phase

1. In the Select Actual Enterprise Phase step, click the Creation Mode .
2. Select the package where you want to create a resource.
3. Click the Create button.
4. In the Specification window of the newly created Actual Enterprise Phase, type its name.

1622433106

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating the SV-9 table' space='UAF12P2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722218 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00918: Resources Information

- page_id: `136722218`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722218/Resources+Information
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:16:50.787+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

915064863

915064865

915064864

**Description**

The Resources Information (Rs-If) domain shows the information perspective on resource architecture. It allows analysis of an architecture’s information and data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation-specific issues.

**Cameo Data Modeler plugin integration**

You can use the Entity Relationship diagram for logical and physical data modeling in the data model diagrams. It allows for using the information engineering notation within this view.

**Implementation**

The Resources Information (Rs-If) view is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Information diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] . It allows analysis of an architecture’s data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation-specific or product-specific issues. Another purpose is to provide a common dictionary of data definitions to consistently express models wherever logical-level resource information elements are included in the descriptions
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Information diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] . It defines the structure of the various kinds of system or service data that are utilized by the systems or services in the Architectural Description. A Physical Data Model diagram is used to describe how the information represented in the Logical Data Model diagram is actually implemented. While the mapping between the logical and physical data models is relatively straightforward, the relationship between the components of each model (e.g., entity types in the logical model versus relational tables in the physical model) is frequently one-to-many or many-to-many.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Entity Relationship Diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] . It supports the information engineering notation.

**Samples**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Logical Data Model

915064862

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Information' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Model' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Model Kind' space='']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Entity Relationship Diagram' space='']

It allows analysis of an architecture’s information and data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation-specific issues.</p><p><strong><span class="fontstyle0">Cameo Data Modeler plugin integration</span></strong></p><p><span class="fontstyle2">You can use the Entity Relationship diagram for logical and physical data modeling in the data model diagrams. It allows for using the information engineering notation within this view.</span></p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Resources Information (Rs-If) view is represented by:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Operational Information diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Logical Data Model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. It allows analysis of an architecture’s data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation-specific or product-specific issues. Another purpose is to provide a common dictionary of data definitions to consistently express models wherever logical-level resource information elements are included in the descriptions</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Resources Information diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Physical Data Model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. It defines the structure of the various kinds of system or service data that are utilized by the systems or services in the Architectural Description. A Physical Data Model diagram is used to describe how the information represented in the Logical Data Model diagram is actually implemented. While the mapping between the logical and physical data models is relatively straightforward, the relationship between the components of each model (e.g., entity types in the logical model versus relational tables in the physical model) is frequently one-to-many or many-to-many.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating Entity Relationship Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Entity Relationship Diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. It supports the information engineering notation.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p><strong>Samples</strong></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Physical Data Model" ac:alt="Physical Data Model"><ri:attachment ri:filename="physical_data_model.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Resources Information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>An example of the Logical Data Model</h6><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fa201259-4ecb-43f4-9d69-f07bb9772fe7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">915064862</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Information" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Information Model" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Information Model Kind" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="2eca6043-525c-4328-b1ed-5f790610911d" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Entity Relationship Diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722296 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00919: Resources Motivation

- page_id: `136722296`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722296/Resources+Motivation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:09.088+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 > Resources
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The **Resources Motivation** (Rq-Mv) view provides a central reference for a set of stakeholder needs expressed as requirements, their relationship (via traceability) to more detailed requirements, and the solution described by the architecture that will meet those requirements. It is used to represent requirements, their properties, and relationships (trace, verify, satisfy, refine) both to each other and to UAF architectural elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The <strong>Resources Motivation</strong> (Rq-Mv) view provides a central reference for a set of stakeholder needs expressed as requirements, their relationship (via traceability) to more detailed requirements, and the solution described by the architecture that will meet those requirements. It is used to represent requirements, their properties, and relationships (trace, verify, satisfy, refine) both to each other and to UAF architectural elements.</p>
````

915386118

**Description**

The Resources Parameters (Rs-Pm) domain depicts the measurements of resources.

The intended usage of the Resources Parameters (Rs-Pm) domain includes:

- Definition of performance characteristics and measurements.
- Identification of non-functional requirements.

**Implementation**

The Resources Parameters (Rs-Pm) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Environment diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Actual Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Parametric diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']
- Actual Measurements (.xlsx).
